steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the entire text. The text opens by posing a question (why do ocelots purr but jaguars roar?), then proposes the hyoid bone structure as a likely explanation for the difference. A main idea answer must capture both the question and its proposed answer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Both ocelots and jaguars have U-shaped hyoid bones, but ocelots' hyoids are rigid whereas jaguars' hyoids are flexible.’: States a supporting detail about hyoid structure, but does not explain why this matters or connect it to the purring/roaring difference. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Researchers are uncertain why relatively small cats, such as ocelots, purr but relatively large cats, such as jaguars, roar.’: Too vague. The passage does frame the idea as a hypothesis, but it still offers a specific hyoid-bone explanation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Jaguars and most other species of big cats can roar because they have flexible hyoid bones that are attached by stretchy ligaments to their skulls.’: Covers the roaring side of the comparison, but omits ocelots, purring, and the full contrast central to the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Differences related to the hyoid bone in ocelots and jaguars may help explain why one species purrs and the other roars.’: Captures both species, the causal role of the hyoid, and the explanatory framing of the passage. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
